title: Intelligence Efficiency, Ben Geist | Compile 26
summary: Ben Geist discusses intelligence efficiency, arguing that token spending shows diminishing returns and that better context, not just increased compute, is the key driver of model efficiency. He frames models as entropy reduction machines and presents evidence from Ramp's token spend data.
writeup: Ben Geist's Compile 26 talk centers on intelligence efficiency, arguing that spending more tokens yields diminishing returns and that better context, rather than added compute alone, drives model efficiency. The framing treats models as entropy reduction machines and draws on token spend data from Ramp as supporting evidence. The takeaway: efficiency gains come from improving the context fed to models, not from scaling token expenditure.
